page: Google Cloud Platform m4n-megamem-112 is a memory-optimized server instance belonging to the m4n server family. It is built on the x86_64 CPU architecture and features 112 dedicated virtual central processing units (vCPUs). The instance is equipped with 1488.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of approximately 13.29 GB per vCPU.

This server configuration does not include local storage or bundled GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage options. Additionally, the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Due to the high density of RAM relative to the compute cores, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for applications requiring massive in-memory datasets where scaling out compute is less critical than maintaining a large memory footprint. Appropriate workloads include large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and complex data analytics pipelines that require substantial memory capacity.
description: Google Cloud Platform m4n-megamem-112 is a memory-optimized server instance from the m4n family, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 112 dedicated vCPUs and 1488.0 GB of RAM, providing a high resource density of 13.29 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external network-attached storage for data persistence. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that demand massive memory capacity without the proportional cost of additional compute cores. It is suited for large-scale in-memory databases, memory-heavy data processing, and enterprise applications.
